I<sup>azwj</sup> Created you<sup>as</sup> from a seed of menial water from clay which I<sup>azwj</sup> Took out from a disgraceful mixture of dust. So it was a human being and I<sup>azwj</sup> carved it into a creature. So Blessed is My<sup>azwj</sup> ‘وَجْهِي’ (My Wali<sup>as</sup>), and Holy is My<sup>azwj</sup> Craft. There is nothing like Me<sup>azwj</sup> and I<sup>azwj</sup> am the Eternal Living One who will not Decline.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! When you supplicate to Me<sup>azwj</sup>, be fearful, anxious and rub your<sup>as</sup> face in the dust to Me<sup>azwj</sup> and prostrate to Me<sup>azwj</sup> by the noble parts of your<sup>as</sup> body, and plead in front of Me<sup>azwj</sup> when standing, and whisper to Me<sup>azwj</sup> when you whisper with a humble heart anxiously, and revive My<sup>azwj</sup> Torah in the days of the life, and teach My<sup>azwj</sup> Praise to the ignorant ones, and remind them of My<sup>azwj</sup> Favours, and My<sup>azwj</sup> Bounties, and tell them not to persist in the transgression in which they are engulfed, for My<sup>azwj</sup> Hold is Painful and Intense.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! If I<sup>azwj</sup> Cut off your Rope from Me<sup>azwj</sup> you<sup>as</sup> will not (be able to) connect with the rope of others. So, worship Me<sup>azwj</sup>, and stand in front of Me<sup>azwj</sup> like the standing of the lowly poor servant. Condemn yourself for it is the highest (most deserving) for condemnation, and do not empower the Children of Israel by My<sup>azwj</sup> Book, for it is sufficient advice for your<sup>as</sup> heart and an enlightenment, and it is the Speech of the Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the Worlds, Majestic and High.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Whenever you supplicate to Me<sup>azwj</sup> and refer to Me<sup>azwj</sup>, I<sup>azwj</sup> shall Forgive you<sup>as</sup> what was from you<sup>as</sup>. The sky Glorifies to Me<sup>azwj</sup> My<sup>azwj</sup> Majesty, and the Angels are fearful of Me<sup>a</sup><sup>zwj</sup> and anxious, and the earth Glorifies to Me<sup>azwj</sup> ravenously, and all of the creation Glorifies Me<sup>azwj</sup> in humbleness. Then it is on you to Pray. The Prayer has a place with Me<sup>azwj</sup> and for it has in My<sup>azwj</sup> Presence a firm Covenant and I<sup>azwj</sup> Attach to it what was from it.

Zakat is a sacrifice in order to cleanse the wealth, and the food, for I<sup>azwj</sup> do not Accept except for the ‘الطَّيِّبَ’ pure, (unless the instructions of) My<sup>azwj</sup> ‘وَجْهِي’ (My Wali<sup>as</sup>) are sought. And I<sup>azwj</sup> Joined with that the maintenance of relations, for I<sup>azwj</sup> am Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, the Beneficent, the Merciful, and as for the relationships, I<sup>azwj</sup> Created it Preferentially from My<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy for the servants to sympathise (with each other) by it, and for it, in My<sup>azwj</sup> Presence is an authorization in the return of the Hereafter, and I<sup>azwj</sup> will Cut-off the one who cuts it off (from My<sup>azwj</sup> Wali<sup>asws</sup>), and Connect with the one who keeps the connection (with My<sup>azwj</sup> Wali<sup>asws</sup>). And that is how I<sup>azwj</sup> will Deal in My<sup>azwj</sup> Command.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Be generous to the beggar when he comes to you<sup>as</sup> by beautiful words, or giving easily because he may have come to you from neither from the humans nor from the Jinn. The Angels of the Beneficent come to test you<sup>as</sup> to see how you<sup>as</sup> are in your<sup>as</sup> dealings in regard to what I<sup>azwj</sup> have Given to you<sup>as</sup>, and how consoling you<sup>as</sup> are regarding what I<sup>azwj</sup> have Authorised you<sup>as</sup> with. And be humble to Me<sup>azwj</sup> with the beseeching, and wail to Me<sup>azwj</sup> by lamentations from the Book. And know that I<sup>azwj</sup> Call you with the Calling of the master to his slave to Make him reach to the honourable position, and that is from My<sup>azwj</sup> Grace to you<sup>as</sup> and to your<sup>as</sup> forefathers of the former ones.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Do not forget Me<sup>azwj</sup> in every situation, and do not be happy due to the abundance of the wealth, because forgetting Me<sup>azwj</sup> hardens the hearts, and the abundance of the wealth brings the abundance of the sins. The earth is obedient, and the sky is obedient, and the oceans are obedient, whereas disobedient to Me<sup>azwj</sup> are the unfortunate ones of Humans and the Jinns (Al-Saqalayn). And I<sup>azwj</sup> am the Beneficent, the Merciful. I<sup>azwj</sup> am Beneficent in every era. I<sup>azwj</sup> Give hardship after ease, and ease after hardship, and kings after kings, whereas My<sup>azwj</sup> Kingdom is permanently based and will never cease. And there is not hidden from Me<sup>azwj</sup> anything in the earth, nor in the sky, and how can it be hidden from Me<sup>azwj</sup> and from Me<sup>azwj</sup> is its beginning, and how come you are not fearful regarding what is in My<sup>azwj</sup> possession, and to Me<sup>azwj</sup> you<sup>as</sup> will inevitably return.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Make Me<sup>azwj</sup> to be your<sup>as</sup> Protector, and place in My<sup>azwj</sup> possession your treasure for the good deeds, and fear Me<sup>azwj</sup> and do not fear others. To Me<sup>azwj</sup> is the destination.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Be merciful to the one who is lower than you among the creatures, and do not become jealous of the one who is above you<sup>as</sup>, for the jealousy consumes the good deeds just like the fire consumes the firewood.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! The two sons of Adam<sup>as</sup> wanted to express humbleness at a stage so that they could win My<sup>azwj</sup> Grace and My<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy. So they offered offerings, and I<sup>azwj</sup> do not Accept except from the pious ones. So their affair was what you<sup>as</sup> know, so, after this, how can you<sup>as</sup> (select) a trustworthy companion, the brother and the vizier (but by Me<sup>azwj</sup>)?
O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Put aside the arrogance and leave the pride, and remember that you<sup>as</sup> will be dwelling in the grave, and that should prevent you<sup>as</sup> from the lustful desires.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Hasten to the repentance, and delay the sins, and deliberate in your<sup>as</sup> staying in front of Me<sup>azwj</sup> during the Prayer, and do not place hope in others. Take Me<sup>azwj</sup> to be your<sup>as</sup> shield in the difficulties, and as a fortress in disastrous circumstances.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! How can a creature humble itself to Me<sup>azwj</sup> without being aware of My<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy, and how can one recognise My<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy without having observed it; and how can one (ever) observe it without having faith in it, and how can one believe in it without having any hope of being blessed from it; and how can one hope for being Blessed while being satisfied with the world, having taken refuge in it and relying on the support of the unjust ones.
O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Compete for the good with the people of goodness because the good is like its name, and leave the evil for those who are fascinated by it.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Keep your<sup>as</sup> tongue behind your<sup>as</sup> heart and you<sup>as</sup> will be safe, and Remember Me<sup>azwj</sup> abundantly during the night and the day and you will gain, and do not follow the sins (errors) but you<sup>as</sup> will regret, for the sins are scheduled for the Fire.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Speak good to the people who have left the sins and be in their company, and take them as your<sup>as</sup> brother in your<sup>as</sup> absence, and strive along with them and let them strive with you<sup>as</sup>.
O Musa<sup>as</sup>! The death will inevitably come to you<sup>as</sup>. So make provision of such provisions like the one who is certain that it would last him till (he reaches) his destination.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! What you<sup>as</sup> intend with by for My<sup>azwj</sup> Face ‘وَجْهِي’ (My Wali<sup>as</sup>), even a little (submission) is a lot, and what you<sup>as</sup> intend with by for other than Me<sup>azwj</sup>, even a lot (of effort to please him) is little (worthless). And reform your<sup>as</sup> days which are in front of you<sup>a</sup><sup>s</sup>, and to realize the day in front of you and prepare to benefit from it, as for you<sup>as</sup> will be Paused and Questioned. Take your<sup>as</sup> lesson from the (past) era and its people, for the span of the era is short, but its shortness can be availed (to earn rewards), since everything is destined for destruction. So act as if you<sup>as</sup> can see the Reward for your<sup>as</sup> deeds in order for it to be an inevitable greed for you<sup>as</sup> regarding the Hereafter, for whatever is remaining from the world is like what has passed from it, and every worker strives upon visions and examples. So return to yourself<sup>as</sup>, O son<sup>as</sup> of Imran<sup>as</sup>, perhaps you<sup>as</sup> will succeed tomorrow on the Day of Questioning, for that is where the wrong doers will lose out.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Throw out your<sup>as</sup> hand in front of Me<sup>azwj</sup> like the act of the slave calling out to his master, for if you<sup>as</sup> were to do that, you<sup>as</sup> will be the recipient of Mercy for I<sup>azwj</sup> am the Most Generous of the Powerful ones.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Ask Me<sup>azwj</sup> from My<sup>azwj</sup> Grace, and My<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy, for these two are in My<sup>azwj</sup> Hands, none other is their owner, and look when you<sup>as</sup> ask Me<sup>azwj</sup> how your<sup>as</sup> wish is with what is in My<sup>azwj</sup> Possession. For every worker is a Recompense, and the disbeliever will be Recompensed with what he strives for.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Do not concern yourself<sup>as</sup> with the world and seclude from it, for it is not for you<sup>as</sup>, and you<sup>as</sup> are not its owner, and it is a house for the unjust ones. However, the one who does good deeds in it, it is a good house for him.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! What I<sup>azwj</sup> have Commanded you<sup>as</sup> to, so listen and keep quiet at whatever you<sup>as</sup> see. Take the realities of the Torah to your<sup>as</sup> chest, and keep vigil by it in the hours of the night and the day, and do not let the sons of the world to make your<sup>as</sup> chest to be a nest like the nest of the birds.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! The sons of the world and its people are a trial for some of them to the others. So each one of them decorates what is regarding him, whereas the Believer is the one for whom the Hereafter is decorated. So he looks at it unabated, and its pleasures become a barrier for him in between him and the pleasures of life. So he idealises by the dawn like the act of the passenger driven by it to its destination, remaining bleak and sad in the evening. So good news would be for him, and the curtain is Lifted, what he will see would delight his eyes.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! The world is a seed. It is not of any benefit for the Believer, nor is it a penalty for the one who is sinful. So, a lengthy woe is for the one who sells the Reward of his return for a morsel, which does not remain and a sip, which does not continue. So be as I<sup>azwj</sup> have Commanded you<sup>as</sup> to be, and every Command of Mine<sup>azwj</sup> is Guidance.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! When you<sup>as</sup> see the richness coming to you<sup>as</sup>, so say: ‘It is a sin which is hastening the punishment for me<sup>as</sup>’. And when you<sup>as</sup> see the poverty coming to you<sup>as</sup>, so say: ‘Welcome to the sign of the righteous ones’. And do not become an unjust tyrant, and do not join up with the unjust ones.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! What is a lifetime and it is lengthy and its end is condemnable. And what goes away from you<sup>as</sup> does not adversely affect you<sup>as</sup> if you<sup>a</sup><sup>s</sup> were to praise its result.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! The Book has Screamed out to you<sup>as</sup> a loud Scream with what you<sup>as</sup> are headed for. So how can the eyes fall asleep upon this, or how can the people find pleasure in the life. Had it not been for the deliberately being oblivious, and the following of the misfortune and the following of the lustful desires, and from other than this, that the righteous are in grief.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Order My<sup>azwj</sup> servants to supplicate to Me<sup>azwj</sup> in whatever situation they may be in after having accepted that I<sup>a</sup><sup>zwj</sup> am indeed the Most Merciful of the merciful ones, the Answerer to the restless, and Remover of the evil, and the Changer of the eras, and the Giver of comfort, Thankful for a little, and Rewarding a great deal, and Enricher of the poor, and I<sup>azwj</sup> am the Eternally Mighty and Powerful. So the one who comes to you<sup>as</sup> and joined up with you<sup>as</sup> from the sinful ones, so say: ‘Welcome to the courtyard of the Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the Worlds’, and seek Forgiven for them, and be kind to them like one of other, and do not dominate them by what I<sup>azwj</sup> have Given to you<sup>as</sup> of the preference over them, and tell them to ask from Me<sup>azwj</sup> from (and) My<sup>azwj</sup> Grace, and My<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy, for no one else is the owner of these, and I<sup>azwj</sup> am of the Great Grace , Good to you.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Be a cave for the erroneous, and sit with the restless, and a seeker of Forgiveness among the sinful ones. You<sup>as</sup> are from Me<sup>azwj</sup> at the status of the Pleasure, so supplicate to Me<sup>azwj</sup> with the pure heart, and the truthful tongue, and be as I<sup>azwj</sup> have Commanded you<sup>as</sup> to be. Be obedient to My<sup>azwj</sup> Command and do not dominate over My<sup>azwj</sup> servants by what is not begun from you<sup>as</sup>. And come nearer to Me<sup>azwj</sup>, for I<sup>azwj</sup> am very near to you<sup>as</sup>, for I<sup>azwj</sup> will never Ask you<sup>as</sup> what injures you<sup>as</sup> by its weight nor to carry it (burden). But rather, I<sup>azwj</sup> Ask you<sup>as</sup> that you<sup>as</sup> should supplicate to Me<sup>azwj</sup> so I<sup>azwj</sup> will Answer you<sup>as</sup>, and that Ask from Me<sup>azwj</sup> so I<sup>azwj</sup> shall Give it to you<sup>as</sup>, and that to come near to Me<sup>az</sup><sup>wj</sup> by what you<sup>as</sup> have taken from Me<sup>azwj</sup> of its interpretation, and to Me<sup>azwj</sup> is the completion of its Revelation.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! Look towards the earth, for very soon it shall be your<sup>as</sup> grave, and raise your<sup>as</sup> eyes towards the sky for it is above you<sup>as</sup> and in it are great Angels, and weep upon yourself<sup>as</sup> for as long as you<sup>as</sup> are in the world, and fear the perishable and the destruction, and do not be deceived by the adornments of the world and its flowers, and do not be pleased with the injustice, and do not become an unjust one for I<sup>azwj</sup> Hold the oppressor to Account until I<sup>azwj</sup> Get justice from him for the oppressed one.

O Musa<sup>as</sup>! The good deeds are Rewarded tenfold but there is single punishment for a sin even one is so severe. Do not Associate (anyone) with Me<sup>azwj</sup>, it is not Permissible for you that you<sup>as</sup> should Associate with Me<sup>azwj</sup>. Come nearer and be steadfast, and supplicate like the supplication of the greedy one greedy for what is in My<sup>azwj</sup> Possession, and regretful over what he has sent by what his hands (had earned) before. Just as the blackness of the night obliterates the day, and similarly the sins obliterate the good deeds, and the darkness of the night comes over the whiteness of the day, and similarly the sins come over the outstanding sins and blacken them (further)’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Amro Bin Usman, from Ali Bin Isa, from an unbroken chain. He (a.s.) said: ‘ (Prophet) Musa (as) was whispering (Munajaat) to Allah (azwj) Blessed and High. He (azwj) Said in His (azwj) Whispering: ‘O Musa (as)! Do not have long hopes regarding the world. That would harden your (as) heart, and the hard-hearted one is remote from Me (azwj). O Musa (as)! Turn yourself (as) (to obedience) for the sake of My (azwj) Pleasure, for if My (azwj) Pleasure is obeyed, so I (azwj) am not disobeyed. Kill your heart with the fear and create new clothes for the heart. You (as) will be hidden from the people of the earth and be recognised by the people of the sky. Spread out the lamps in your house at night and plead before Me (azwj) with the pleading of the patient ones and wail to Me (azwj) from the great number of the sins like the wailing of the sinner who has fled from his enemies. And seek help for that from Me (azwj) for I (azwj) am the Best of the Helpers, and the best of the Supporters. O Musa (as)! I (azwj) am Allah (azwj) high above the servants, and the servants are below Me (azwj), and everyone is helpless before Me (azwj). Accuse yourself for having wronged yourself, and do not trust your (as) son upon your (as) Religion unless he becomes like you (as), loving the righteous ones. O Musa (as)! Wash and bathe and be near to My (azwj) righteous servants. O Musa (as)! Be their Imam in their Prayers, and their Imam in what they squabble about, and judge between them by what I (azwj) have Revealed unto you (as). I (azwj) have Sent it down as a clear and luminous Proof, and a Light Speaking by what had transpired regarding the former ones and what will be regarding the later ones. I (azwj) Bequeath to you (as), O Musa (as), a bequest of the compassionate, the kind-hearted son of the Pious Lady, Isa (as) son of Maryam (as), owner of the mule, and the hooded gown, and the oil and the olive, and the Prayer Niche. And from after him (saww), about the owner of the red camel, the food, and pure, the purified. His (saww) example in your (as) Book is that he (saww) is a Believer, dominant over all the Books, and that he (saww) is the bowing one, the prostrating one, a seeker, a Monk, his (saww) brothers will be the poor, and his (saww) helpers will be another people, and there will occur in his (saww) era hardships and earthquakes, killings, and shortages of the wealth. His (saww) name would be Ahmad (saww), Muhammad (saww), the trusted one from the remaining ones of the previous generations. He (saww) will believe in all the Books and ratify all the Messengers (as) and testify sincerely for all the Prophets (as). His (saww) community will be the ones under the Mercy, Blessed with what will remain in the Religion upon its reality. They will fulfill their Prayers at its prescribed times, as servants optionally do for their chiefs, acknowledge him (saww) and follow his (saww) path. So he (saww) is your (as) brother. O Musa (as)! He (saww) is the ‘Ummy’, and he (saww) is a truthful servant. Whatever he (saww) places his (saww) hand upon, it becomes a Blessing for him (saww) and a Blessing for it. That is how it was in My (saww) Knowledge and that is how I (azwj) Created him (saww) as. It is by him (saww) that I (azwj) will Open the Hour (Day of Judgement) and it is by his (saww) community I (azwj) shall Seal (Bring an end to) the keys of the world. So, order the unjust ones of the Children of Israel that they should learn his (saww) name, and not to abandon it, but they (surely) will do it. And to love him (saww) is a good deed to Me (azwj), for I (azwj) am with him (saww) and I (azwj) am from his (saww) group, and he (saww) is from My (azwj) Group, and their group shall overcome. So, I (azwj) have Completed My (azwj) Words and will Make his (saww) Religion to prevail over all the Religions, and I (azwj) will be worshipped in all places, and I (azwj) shall Reveal unto him (saww) the Quran as a Criterion, a healing for what is in the chests from the whisperings of Satan (la). So send greetings to him (saww), O son (as) of Imran (as), for I (azwj) Send Greetings to him (saww) and so do My (azwj) Angels. O Musa (as)! You (as) are My (azwj) servant and I (azwj) am your (azwj) God. Do not belittle the despicable, the poor, and do not envy the rich for a little thing. And become humble in My (azwj) Remembrance and in it’s (the Torah) Recitation ravenously by My (azwj) Mercy, and make Me (azwj) Hear the pleasance of the Torah in a humble voice, full of grief. Be at rest during My (azwj) Remembrance, and mention Me (azwj) to the ‘one’ who is assured to Me (azwj), and worship Me (azwj) and do not associate with Me (azwj) anything. And make haste to My (azwj) Pleasure, ‘إِنِّي أَنَا السَّيِّدُ الْكَبِيرُ’ for I (azwj) and the Great Master (azwj). I (azwj) Created you (as) from a seed of menial water from clay which I (azwj) Took out from a disgraceful mixture of dust. So it was a human being and I (azwj) carved it into a creature. So Blessed is My (azwj) ‘وَجْهِي’ (My Wali (as)), and Holy is My (azwj) Craft. There is nothing like Me (azwj) and I (azwj) am the Eternal Living One who will not Decline. O Musa (as)! When you supplicate to Me (azwj), be fearful, anxious and rub your (as) face in the dust to Me (azwj) and prostrate to Me (azwj) by the noble parts of your (as) body, and plead in front of Me (azwj) when standing, and whisper to Me (azwj) when you whisper with a humble heart anxiously, and revive My (azwj) Torah in the days of the life, and teach My (azwj) Praise to the ignorant ones, and remind them of My (azwj) Favours, and My (azwj) Bounties, and tell them not to persist in the transgression in which they are engulfed, for My (azwj) Hold is Painful and Intense. O Musa (as)! If I (azwj) Cut off your Rope from Me (azwj) you (as) will not (be able to) connect with the rope of others. So, worship Me (azwj), and stand in front of Me (azwj) like the standing of the lowly poor servant. Condemn yourself for it is the highest (most deserving) for condemnation, and do not empower the Children of Israel by My (azwj) Book, for it is sufficient advice for your (as) heart and an enlightenment, and it is the Speech of the Lord (azwj) of the Worlds, Majestic and High. O Musa (as)! Whenever you supplicate to Me (azwj) and refer to Me (azwj), I (azwj) shall Forgive you (as) what was from you (as). The sky Glorifies to Me (azwj) My (azwj) Majesty, and the Angels are fearful of Me (azwj) and anxious, and the earth Glorifies to Me (azwj) ravenously, and all of the creation Glorifies Me (azwj) in humbleness. Then it is on you to Pray. The Prayer has a place with Me (azwj) and for it has in My (azwj) Presence a firm Covenant and I (azwj) Attach to it what was from it. Zakat is a sacrifice in order to cleanse the wealth, and the food, for I (azwj) do not Accept except for the ‘الطَّيِّبَ’ pure, (unless the instructions of) My (azwj) ‘وَجْهِي’ (My Wali (as)) are sought. And I (azwj) Joined with that the maintenance of relations, for I (azwj) am Allah (azwj), the Beneficent, the Merciful, and as for the relationships, I (azwj) Created it Preferentially from My (azwj) Mercy for the servants to sympathise (with each other) by it, and for it, in My (azwj) Presence is an authorization in the return of the Hereafter, and I (azwj) will Cut-off the one who cuts it off (from My (azwj) Wali (a.s.)), and Connect with the one who keeps the connection (with My (azwj) Wali (a.s.)). And that is how I (azwj) will Deal in My (azwj) Command. O Musa (as)! Be generous to the beggar when he comes to you (as) by beautiful words, or giving easily because he may have come to you from neither from the humans nor from the Jinn. The Angels of the Beneficent come to test you (as) to see how you (as) are in your (as) dealings in regard to what I (azwj) have Given to you (as), and how consoling you (as) are regarding what I (azwj) have Authorised you (as) with. And be humble to Me (azwj) with the beseeching, and wail to Me (azwj) by lamentations from the Book. And know that I (azwj) Call you with the Calling of the master to his slave to Make him reach to the honourable position, and that is from My (azwj) Grace to you (as) and to your (as) forefathers of the former ones. O Musa (as)! Do not forget Me (azwj) in every situation, and do not be happy due to the abundance of the wealth, because forgetting Me (azwj) hardens the hearts, and the abundance of the wealth brings the abundance of the sins. The earth is obedient, and the sky is obedient, and the oceans are obedient, whereas disobedient to Me (azwj) are the unfortunate ones of Humans and the Jinns (Al-Saqalayn). And I (azwj) am the Beneficent, the Merciful. I (azwj) am Beneficent in every era. I (azwj) Give hardship after ease, and ease after hardship, and kings after kings, whereas My (azwj) Kingdom is permanently based and will never cease. And there is not hidden from Me (azwj) anything in the earth, nor in the sky, and how can it be hidden from Me (azwj) and from Me (azwj) is its beginning, and how come you are not fearful regarding what is in My (azwj) possession, and to Me (azwj) you (as) will inevitably return. O Musa (as)! Make Me (azwj) to be your (as) Protector, and place in My (azwj) possession your treasure for the good deeds, and fear Me (azwj) and do not fear others. To Me (azwj) is the destination. O Musa (as)! Be merciful to the one who is lower than you among the creatures, and do not become jealous of the one who is above you (as), for the jealousy consumes the good deeds just like the fire consumes the firewood. O Musa (as)! The two sons of Adam (as) wanted to express humbleness at a stage so that they could win My (azwj) Grace and My (azwj) Mercy. So they offered offerings, and I (azwj) do not Accept except from the pious ones. So their affair was what you (as) know, so, after this, how can you (as) (select) a trustworthy companion, the brother and the vizier (but by Me (azwj))? O Musa (as)! Put aside the arrogance and leave the pride, and remember that you (as) will be dwelling in the grave, and that should prevent you (as) from the lustful desires. O Musa (as)! Hasten to the repentance, and delay the sins, and deliberate in your (as) staying in front of Me (azwj) during the Prayer, and do not place hope in others. Take Me (azwj) to be your (as) shield in the difficulties, and as a fortress in disastrous circumstances. O Musa (as)! How can a creature humble itself to Me (azwj) without being aware of My (azwj) Mercy, and how can one recognise My (azwj) Mercy without having observed it; and how can one (ever) observe it without having faith in it, and how can one believe in it without having any hope of being blessed from it; and how can one hope for being Blessed while being satisfied with the world, having taken refuge in it and relying on the support of the unjust ones. O Musa (as)! Compete for the good with the people of goodness because the good is like its name, and leave the evil for those who are fascinated by it. O Musa (as)! Keep your (as) tongue behind your (as) heart and you (as) will be safe, and Remember Me (azwj) abundantly during the night and the day and you will gain, and do not follow the sins (errors) but you (as) will regret, for the sins are scheduled for the Fire. O Musa (as)! Speak good to the people who have left the sins and be in their company, and take them as your (as) brother in your (as) absence, and strive along with them and let them strive with you (as). O Musa (as)! The death will inevitably come to you (as). So make provision of such provisions like the one who is certain that it would last him till (he reaches) his destination. O Musa (as)! What you (as) intend with by for My (azwj) Face ‘وَجْهِي’ (My Wali (as)), even a little (submission) is a lot, and what you (as) intend with by for other than Me (azwj), even a lot (of effort to please him) is little (worthless). And reform your (as) days which are in front of you (as), and to realize the day in front of you and prepare to benefit from it, as for you (as) will be Paused and Questioned. Take your (as) lesson from the (past) era and its people, for the span of the era is short, but its shortness can be availed (to earn rewards), since everything is destined for destruction. So act as if you (as) can see the Reward for your (as) deeds in order for it to be an inevitable greed for you (as) regarding the Hereafter, for whatever is remaining from the world is like what has passed from it, and every worker strives upon visions and examples. So return to yourself (as), O son (as) of Imran (as), perhaps you (as) will succeed tomorrow on the Day of Questioning, for that is where the wrong doers will lose out. O Musa (as)! Throw out your (as) hand in front of Me (azwj) like the act of the slave calling out to his master, for if you (as) were to do that, you (as) will be the recipient of Mercy for I (azwj) am the Most Generous of the Powerful ones. O Musa (as)! Ask Me (azwj) from My (azwj) Grace, and My (azwj) Mercy, for these two are in My (azwj) Hands, none other is their owner, and look when you (as) ask Me (azwj) how your (as) wish is with what is in My (azwj) Possession. For every worker is a Recompense, and the disbeliever will be Recompensed with what he strives for. O Musa (as)! Do not concern yourself (as) with the world and seclude from it, for it is not for you (as), and you (as) are not its owner, and it is a house for the unjust ones. However, the one who does good deeds in it, it is a good house for him. O Musa (as)! What I (azwj) have Commanded you (as) to, so listen and keep quiet at whatever you (as) see. Take the realities of the Torah to your (as) chest, and keep vigil by it in the hours of the night and the day, and do not let the sons of the world to make your (as) chest to be a nest like the nest of the birds. O Musa (as)! The sons of the world and its people are a trial for some of them to the others. So each one of them decorates what is regarding him, whereas the Believer is the one for whom the Hereafter is decorated. So he looks at it unabated, and its pleasures become a barrier for him in between him and the pleasures of life. So he idealises by the dawn like the act of the passenger driven by it to its destination, remaining bleak and sad in the evening. So good news would be for him, and the curtain is Lifted, what he will see would delight his eyes. O Musa (as)! The world is a seed. It is not of any benefit for the Believer, nor is it a penalty for the one who is sinful. So, a lengthy woe is for the one who sells the Reward of his return for a morsel, which does not remain and a sip, which does not continue. So be as I (azwj) have Commanded you (as) to be, and every Command of Mine (azwj) is Guidance. O Musa (as)! When you (as) see the richness coming to you (as), so say: ‘It is a sin which is hastening the punishment for me (as)’. And when you (as) see the poverty coming to you (as), so say: ‘Welcome to the sign of the righteous ones’. And do not become an unjust tyrant, and do not join up with the unjust ones. O Musa (as)! What is a lifetime and it is lengthy and its end is condemnable. And what goes away from you (as) does not adversely affect you (as) if you (as) were to praise its result. O Musa (as)! The Book has Screamed out to you (as) a loud Scream with what you (as) are headed for. So how can the eyes fall asleep upon this, or how can the people find pleasure in the life. Had it not been for the deliberately being oblivious, and the following of the misfortune and the following of the lustful desires, and from other than this, that the righteous are in grief. O Musa (as)! Order My (azwj) servants to supplicate to Me (azwj) in whatever situation they may be in after having accepted that I (azwj) am indeed the Most Merciful of the merciful ones, the Answerer to the restless, and Remover of the evil, and the Changer of the eras, and the Giver of comfort, Thankful for a little, and Rewarding a great deal, and Enricher of the poor, and I (azwj) am the Eternally Mighty and Powerful. So the one who comes to you (as) and joined up with you (as) from the sinful ones, so say: ‘Welcome to the courtyard of the Lord (azwj) of the Worlds’, and seek Forgiven for them, and be kind to them like one of other, and do not dominate them by what I (azwj) have Given to you (as) of the preference over them, and tell them to ask from Me (azwj) from (and) My (azwj) Grace, and My (azwj) Mercy, for no one else is the owner of these, and I (azwj) am of the Great Grace , Good to you. O Musa (as)! Be a cave for the erroneous, and sit with the restless, and a seeker of Forgiveness among the sinful ones. You (as) are from Me (azwj) at the status of the Pleasure, so supplicate to Me (azwj) with the pure heart, and the truthful tongue, and be as I (azwj) have Commanded you (as) to be. Be obedient to My (azwj) Command and do not dominate over My (azwj) servants by what is not begun from you (as). And come nearer to Me (azwj), for I (azwj) am very near to you (as), for I (azwj) will never Ask you (as) what injures you (as) by its weight nor to carry it (burden). But rather, I (azwj) Ask you (as) that you (as) should supplicate to Me (azwj) so I (azwj) will Answer you (as), and that Ask from Me (azwj) so I (azwj) shall Give it to you (as), and that to come near to Me (azwj) by what you (as) have taken from Me (azwj) of its interpretation, and to Me (azwj) is the completion of its Revelation. O Musa (as)! Look towards the earth, for very soon it shall be your (as) grave, and raise your (as) eyes towards the sky for it is above you (as) and in it are great Angels, and weep upon yourself (as) for as long as you (as) are in the world, and fear the perishable and the destruction, and do not be deceived by the adornments of the world and its flowers, and do not be pleased with the injustice, and do not become an unjust one for I (azwj) Hold the oppressor to Account until I (azwj) Get justice from him for the oppressed one. O Musa (as)! The good deeds are Rewarded tenfold but there is single punishment for a sin even one is so severe. Do not Associate (anyone) with Me (azwj), it is not Permissible for you that you (as) should Associate with Me (azwj). Come nearer and be steadfast, and supplicate like the supplication of the greedy one greedy for what is in My (azwj) Possession, and regretful over what he has sent by what his hands (had earned) before. Just as the blackness of the night obliterates the day, and similarly the sins obliterate the good deeds, and the darkness of the night comes over the whiteness of the day, and similarly the sins come over the outstanding sins and blacken them (further)’.

translations.bn: আমি আবু আবদিল্লাহ (আলাইহিস সালাম)-এর পক্ষ থেকে তাঁর এক সাথীকে লেখা একটি জবাব পড়লাম। আম্মা বা'দ:
translations.de: Ich las eine Antwort von Abu Abdillah (Friede sei mit ihm) an einen Mann aus seinen Gefährten. Nach [dem Gotteslob]:
translations.en: I read a reply from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) to a man from his companions. After this [having praised God]:
translations.es: Leí una respuesta de Abu Abdillah (paz sea con él) a un hombre de sus compañeros. Después:
translations.fa: جوابی از ابو عبدالله علیه السلام به مردی از اصحابش خواندم. اما بعد:
translations.fr: J'ai lu une réponse d'Abu Abdillah (paix sur lui) à un homme de ses compagnons. Après le [louange à Dieu] :
translations.id: Aku membaca sebuah balasan dari Abu Abdillah (alayhis salam) kepada seorang laki-laki dari para sahabatnya. Amma ba'd:
translations.ru: Я прочитал ответное письмо Абу Абдиллаха (мир ему) одному из его сподвижников. После [восхваления Аллаха]:
translations.tr: Ebu Abdullah'tan (aleyhisselam) ashabından bir adama yazılmış bir cevabı okudum. Hamd'in ardından:
translations.ur: میں نے ابو عبداللہ علیہ السلام کی طرف سے ان کے ایک صحابی کو لکھا گیا جواب پڑھا۔ اما بعد:
translations.zh: 我读到了一封阿布·阿卜迪拉（愿主赐福他）写给一位弟子的回信。感赞主后：

translations.bn: আমি তোমাকে আল্লাহর তাকওয়ার উপদেশ দিচ্ছি; কেননা আল্লাহ তাঁর যে বান্দা তাঁকে ভয় করে তার জন্য এই দায়িত্ব নিয়েছেন যে তাকে তিনি যা অপছন্দ করেন তা থেকে তিনি যা ভালোবাসেন সেদিকে ফিরিয়ে দেবেন এবং তাকে এমন জায়গা থেকে রিযিক দেবেন যেখান থেকে সে ধারণাও করে না।
translations.de: Ich rate dir zur Gottesfurcht (Taqwa); denn wahrlich hat Gott dem, der Ihn fürchtet, verbürgt, ihn von dem, was Er verabscheut, hin zu dem, was Er liebt, zu wenden, und ihn von dort zu versorgen, wo er es nicht erwartet.
translations.en: I hereby advise you to fear God; for God has taken the responsibility for whoever is pious toward Him to turn them from what He abhors toward what He loves, and to provide for them from where they do not expect.
translations.es: Te aconsejo la taqwa de Dios; pues ciertamente Dios ha garantizado para quien Le teme que lo transformará de lo que Él aborrece hacia lo que ama, y le proveerá de donde no lo espera.
translations.fa: تو را به تقوای الهی سفارش می‌کنم، زیرا خداوند برای کسی که از او پروا کند ضمانت داده است که او را از آنچه کراهت دارد به آنچه دوست دارد منتقل کند و از جایی که گمان نمی‌برد روزی‌اش دهد.
translations.fr: Je te recommande la taqwa de Dieu ; car certes Dieu a garanti pour celui qui Le craint de le détourner de ce qu'Il déteste vers ce qu'Il aime, et de le pourvoir depuis là où il ne s'y attend pas.
translations.id: Aku berwasiat kepadamu dengan takwa kepada Allah; karena sesungguhnya Allah telah menjamin bagi siapa yang bertakwa kepada-Nya untuk mengalihkannya dari apa yang Dia benci menuju apa yang Dia cintai, dan memberinya rezeki dari tempat yang tidak ia sangka.
translations.ru: Я завещаю тебе богобоязненность (таква); ибо поистине Аллах взял на себя ответственность за того, кто Его боится, — обратить его от того, что Он ненавидит, к тому, что Он любит, и наделить его уделом оттуда, откуда тот не ожидает.
translations.tr: Sana Allah'a karşı takvayı tavsiye ediyorum; zira Allah, kendisine karşı takvalı olan kimse için O'nun hoşlanmadığından, sevdiğine dönüştüreceğini ve onu hiç beklemediği yerden rızıklandıracağını garanti etmiştir.
translations.ur: میں تمہیں اللہ کی تقویٰ کی وصیت کرتا ہوں، کیونکہ اللہ نے اس شخص کے لیے ضمانت لی ہے جو اس سے ڈرے کہ اسے جو چیز وہ ناپسند کرتا ہے اس سے پھیر کر جو وہ پسند کرتا ہے اس طرف لے جائے گا، اور اسے وہاں سے رزق دے گا جہاں سے وہ گمان بھی نہیں کرتا۔
translations.zh: 我嘱咐你敬畏主（塔格瓦）；因为确实，安拉对于敬畏祂者已做出保证，将他从祂所厌恶的转向祂所喜爱的，并从他所预想不到的地方给养他。

translations.bn: সুতরাং সাবধান হও যাতে তুমি তাদের মধ্যে না পড়ো যারা বান্দাদের গুনাহের কারণে তাদের জন্য ভয় পায় অথচ নিজের গুনাহের শাস্তি থেকে নিজেকে নিরাপদ মনে করে। কেননা আল্লাহ আয্যা ওয়া জাল্লা তাঁর জান্নাত সম্পর্কে প্রতারিত হন না এবং তাঁর কাছে যা আছে তা তাঁর আনুগত্য ছাড়া পাওয়া যায় না, ইনশাআল্লাহ।
translations.de: Hüte dich also davor, zu jenen zu gehören, die um die Diener [Gottes] wegen ihrer Sünden fürchten, während sie sich selbst vor der Strafe für ihre eigene Sünde sicher fühlen. Denn wahrlich kann Gott, der Mächtige und Erhabene, bezüglich Seines Paradieses nicht getäuscht werden, und was bei Ihm ist, kann nur durch Gehorsam Ihm gegenüber erlangt werden, so Gott will.
translations.en: So beware of being among those who fear for [other] servants [of God] because of their sins while feeling themselves safe from punishment for their own sins. For God, Mighty and Majestic, cannot be deceived concerning His Paradise, nor can what is with Him be attained except by obedience to Him, God willing.
translations.es: Así que cuídate de ser de quienes temen por los siervos [de Dios] debido a sus pecados mientras que ellos mismos se sienten seguros del castigo por sus propios pecados. Pues ciertamente Dios, Poderoso y Majestuoso, no puede ser engañado respecto a Su Paraíso, ni puede alcanzarse lo que está junto a Él excepto por obediencia a Él, si Dios quiere.
translations.fa: پس بپرهیز از اینکه از کسانی باشی که از گناهان بندگان بر آنان می‌ترسند اما خود از مجازات گناه خود در امانند. زیرا خداوند عزوجل درباره بهشتش فریب نمی‌خورد و آنچه نزد اوست جز با طاعتش به دست نمی‌آید، ان شاء الله.
translations.fr: Alors garde-toi d'être de ceux qui craignent pour les serviteurs [de Dieu] à cause de leurs péchés tout en se sentant eux-mêmes à l'abri du châtiment pour leurs propres péchés. Car certes Dieu, Puissant et Majestueux, ne peut être trompé concernant Son Paradis, et ce qui est chez Lui ne peut être atteint que par l'obéissance à Lui, si Dieu le veut.
translations.id: Maka waspadalah untuk tidak menjadi dari golongan yang takut terhadap para hamba karena dosa-dosa mereka, namun merasa aman dari hukuman atas dosanya sendiri. Karena sesungguhnya Allah 'Azza wa Jalla tidak dapat ditipu tentang surga-Nya, dan apa yang ada di sisi-Nya tidak dapat dicapai kecuali dengan ketaatan kepada-Nya, insya Allah.
translations.ru: Остерегайся быть из тех, кто боится за рабов [Аллаха] из-за их грехов, при этом сам чувствует себя в безопасности от наказания за собственный грех. Ибо поистине Аллах, Всемогущий и Величественный, не может быть обманут относительно Своего Рая, и то, что у Него, может быть достигнуто лишь повиновением Ему, если пожелает Аллах.
translations.tr: Öyleyse başkalarının günahları için onlara ağlayıp kendi günahının cezasından güvende hissedenlerin arasından olmaktan kaçın. Çünkü Allah Azze ve Celle, cenneti konusunda aldatılamaz ve O'nun katındakiler ancak O'na itaatle elde edilebilir, inşallah.
translations.ur: پس خبردار رہو کہ تم ان لوگوں میں سے نہ ہو جائو جو بندوں کے گناہوں سے ان کے لیے ڈرتے ہیں مگر خود اپنے گناہ کی سزا سے بے خوف ہیں۔ کیونکہ اللہ عزوجل کو اس کی جنت کے بارے میں دھوکہ نہیں دیا جا سکتا اور اس کے پاس جو کچھ ہے وہ اس کی اطاعت کے سوا حاصل نہیں ہو سکتا، ان شاء اللہ۔
translations.zh: 所以要警惕成为那些担心众人因其罪行而获罪，却对自己的罪行之惩罚浑然不觉的人。因为确实，安拉尊威无比，祂不会被人在祂的乐园问题上欺骗，祂那里的一切也只有通过服从祂才能获得，若主意欲。

diacritics_changes: Added full tashkeel to the Arabic text throughout
Separated وَ from يَرْزُقَهُ and يَأْمَنُ for proper word segmentation

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَمَّنْ ذَكَرَهُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ وَحُمَيْدُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْكِنْدِيِّ جَمِيعاً عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الْمِيثَمِيِّ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: قَرَأْتُ جَوَاباً مِنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِلَى رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ أَمَّا بَعْدُ فَإِنِّي أُوصِيكَ بِتَقْوَى اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ ضَمِنَ لِمَنِ اتَّقَاهُ أَنْ يُحَوِّلَهُ عَمَّا يَكْرَهُ إِلَى مَا يُحِبُّ وَيَرْزُقَهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ فَإِيَّاكَ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِمَّنْ يَخَافُ عَلَى الْعِبَادِ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِمْ وَيَأْمَنُ الْعُقُوبَةَ مِنْ ذَنْبِهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَا يُخْدَعُ عَنْ جَنَّتِهِ وَلَا يُنَالُ مَا عِنْدَهُ إِلَّا بِطَاعَتِهِ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ

narrators.ambiguity_note: Multiple narrators named Ali ibn Muhammad exist in al-Kafi chains
narrators.known_identity: Likely Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Bandhar al-Qummi or another contemporary

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Muhammad ibn al-Husayn ibn Abi al-Khattab, prominent narrator in al-Kafi
narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn al-Husayn ibn Abi al-Khattab
Humayd ibn Ziyad al-Dahinawardi al-Niynawa, a well-known narrator
al-Hasan ibn Muhammad al-Kindi, appears in al-Kafi chains
Ahmad ibn al-Hasan al-Maythami, a narrator from al-Kafi

narrators.ambiguity_note: Anonymous companion of Ahmad ibn al-Hasan al-Maythami; identity unknown

narrators.known_identity: Imam Ja'far ibn Muhammad al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), 6th Imam

key_phrases.phrase_en: After this (amma ba'd) — epistolary/sermon transitional formula
From where one does not expect (divine provision)

bn.تَقْوَى اللَّهِ: আল্লাহর তাকওয়া — আল্লাহর ভয় ও পরহেজগারি
bn.ضَمِنَ: নিশ্চয়তা দিয়েছেন — মুত্তাকিদের জন্য আল্লাহর প্রতিশ্রুতি
bn.لَا يُخْدَعُ: প্রতারিত হন না — আল্লাহকে ভান দিয়ে ধোঁকা দেওয়া যায় না

de.ضَمِنَ: verbürgt — Gottes Versprechen für die Frommen
de.لَا يُخْدَعُ: kann nicht getäuscht werden — Heuchelei täuscht Gott nicht

en.أَمَّا بَعْدُ: amma ba'd — the classical formulaic transition phrase used in letters and sermons after praising God
en.بِطَاعَتِهِ: by obedience to Him — the sole means of attaining divine nearness and Paradise
en.تَقْوَى اللَّهِ: taqwa of God — God-consciousness and piety; the state of mindful devotion and avoidance of what displeases God
en.ضَمِنَ: guaranteed/took responsibility — used to express that God Himself has taken on the obligation to fulfil a promise for the pious
en.لَا يُخْدَعُ عَنْ جَنَّتِهِ: cannot be deceived concerning His Paradise — meaning no outward show of religiosity or self-delusion fools God

es.تَقْوَى: taqwa — piedad y temor de Dios
es.ضَمِنَ: garantizó — la promesa divina para los piadosos
es.لَا يُخْدَعُ عَنْ جَنَّتِهِ: no puede ser engañado sobre Su Paraíso — la hipocresía y el autoengaño no sirven ante Dios

fa.ضَمِنَ: ضمانت داد — تعهد خداوند برای متقیان
fa.لَا يُخْدَعُ عَنْ جَنَّتِهِ: درباره بهشتش فریب نمی‌خورد — ریا و خودفریبی در پیشگاه خدا کارگر نیست

fr.تَقْوَى: taqwa — piété et crainte révérencielle de Dieu
fr.ضَمِنَ: a garanti — la promesse divine pour les pieux
fr.لَا يُخْدَعُ عَنْ جَنَّتِهِ: ne peut être trompé concernant Son Paradis — l'hypocrisie et l'illusion ne trompent pas Dieu

id.تَقْوَى اللَّهِ: takwa kepada Allah — kesadaran dan ketaatan mendalam kepada Allah
id.ضَمِنَ: menjamin — jaminan Allah bagi mereka yang bertakwa
id.لَا يُخْدَعُ: tidak dapat ditipu — Allah tidak tertipu oleh kepura-puraan

ru.ضَمِنَ: взял на себя ответственность — обещание Аллаха для богобоязненных
ru.لَا يُخْدَعُ عَنْ جَنَّتِهِ: не может быть обманут относительно Своего Рая — лицемерие не вводит Аллаха в заблуждение

tr.تَقْوَى: takva — Allah'a karşı derin dindarlık ve sorumluluk bilinci
tr.ضَمِنَ: garanti etti — Allah'ın takvaya sahip olanlar için kesin vaadi
tr.لَا يُخْدَعُ: aldatılamaz — Allah'ın cennetine sahte dindarlıkla ulaşılamaz

ur.بِطَاعَتِهِ: اس کی اطاعت سے — جنت کا واحد ذریعہ
ur.تَقْوَى: تقویٰ — اللہ کا خوف اور پرہیزگاری
ur.ضَمِنَ: ضمانت لی — اللہ نے خود ذمہ داری لی
ur.لَا يُخْدَعُ: دھوکہ نہیں کھاتا — اللہ کو بہانوں سے دھوکا نہیں دیا جا سکتا

zh.تَقْوَى: 塔格瓦 — 敬畏主、虔诚与自律
zh.ضَمِنَ: 保证了 — 安拉对敬畏者的承诺
zh.لَا يُخْدَعُ عَنْ جَنَّتِهِ: 不会被欺骗 — 虚伪和自欺在安拉面前无效

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম সাদিক তাকওয়া ও আল্লাহর গ্যারান্টি সম্পর্কে কী বলেছেন?
seo_questions.de: Was sagte Imam al-Sadiq über Taqwa und Gottes Garantie?
seo_questions.en: What did Imam al-Sadiq say about taqwa and God's guarantee for the pious?
seo_questions.es: ¿Qué dijo el Imam al-Sadiq sobre la taqwa y la garantía de Dios?
seo_questions.fa: امام صادق درباره تقوا و ضمانت خداوند چه فرمودند؟
seo_questions.fr: Qu'a dit l'Imam al-Sadiq sur la taqwa et la garantie de Dieu ?
seo_questions.id: Apa nasihat Imam al-Sadiq tentang takwa dan jaminan Allah?
seo_questions.ru: Что сказал Имам ас-Садик о богобоязненности и гарантии Аллаха?
seo_questions.tr: İmam Sadık takva ve Allah'ın garantisi hakkında ne buyurdu?
seo_questions.ur: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے تقویٰ اور اللہ کی ضمانت کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا؟
seo_questions.zh: 伊玛目萨迪格关于塔格瓦和安拉保证说了什么？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths from Imam al-Sadiq or other Imams emphasizing taqwa as the root of all good in a person, with divine promise of ease and provision for the pious.
Narrations warning against hypocrisy — criticizing others' sins while blind to one's own, as a sign of spiritual disease and self-deception.
Letters or epistles from the Imams to companions, conveying ethical and spiritual counsel in the format of personal correspondence.

summaries.bn: ইমাম সাদিক (আ.) এই পত্রে তাঁর এক সাথীকে তাকওয়াকে আধ্যাত্মিক জীবনের ভিত্তি হিসেবে গ্রহণের নসিহত করেছেন। ইমাম একটি বিপজ্জনক নৈতিক দ্বন্দ্বের দিকে ইঙ্গিত করেছেন: যারা অন্যের গুনাহ নিয়ে উদ্বিগ্ন কিন্তু নিজের গুনাহের পরিণতি থেকে নির্ভয়। আল্লাহর জান্নাত কেবল সত্যিকারের আনুগত্যের মাধ্যমেই অর্জিত হয়।
summaries.de: In diesem Brief rät Imam al-Sadiq (a.s.) einem Gefährten, Taqwa (Gottesfurcht und Frömmigkeit) als Grundlage des geistigen Lebens anzunehmen. Der Imam weist auf einen gefährlichen moralischen Widerspruch hin: jene, die die Sünden anderer fürchten, sich selbst aber vor den Folgen eigener Sünden sicher wähnen. Das Paradies Gottes ist nur durch aufrichtige Gehorsamkeit erlangbar.
summaries.en: In this letter attributed to Imam al-Sadiq (AS), the Imam advises an unnamed companion to embrace taqwa (God-consciousness/piety) as the foundation of spiritual life. The letter highlights a critical moral contradiction: those who are quick to judge others for their sins while remaining blind to their own — a form of self-deception incompatible with genuine faith. The Imam emphasizes that God's Paradise is not attained through wishful thinking but solely through sincere obedience. The passage reflects the genre of epistolary counseling prominent in early Shia tradition, where Imams communicated ethical guidance in writing to distant followers.
summaries.es: En esta carta, el Imam al-Sadiq (P) aconseja a un compañero que adopte la taqwa (piedad/temor de Dios) como base de la vida espiritual. El Imam señala una peligrosa contradicción moral: quienes temen los pecados de los demás mientras se sienten seguros de las consecuencias de los propios. El Paraíso de Dios sólo se alcanza mediante una obediencia genuina.
summaries.fa: امام صادق علیه السلام در این نامه به یکی از اصحابش، تقوا را به عنوان پایه اصلی زندگی معنوی توصیه فرموده است. امام به تناقضی خطرناک اشاره می‌کند: کسی که نگران گناه دیگران است اما از عواقب گناه خود غافل است. جنت الهی تنها با اطاعت واقعی به دست می‌آید.
summaries.fr: Dans cette lettre, l'Imam al-Sadiq (P) conseille à un compagnon d'adopter la taqwa (piété/crainte de Dieu) comme fondement de la vie spirituelle. L'Imam souligne une contradiction morale dangereuse : ceux qui redoutent les péchés des autres tout en se sentant eux-mêmes hors de portée des conséquences de leurs propres péchés. Le Paradis de Dieu ne peut être atteint que par une obéissance sincère.
summaries.id: Imam al-Sadiq (AS) dalam surat ini menasihati seorang sahabatnya tentang pentingnya takwa sebagai fondasi kehidupan spiritual. Beliau mengingatkan tentang kontradiksi moral berbahaya: orang yang mengkhawatirkan dosa orang lain namun merasa aman dari konsekuensi dosanya sendiri. Surga Allah hanya dapat diraih melalui ketaatan yang tulus.
summaries.ru: В этом письме Имам ас-Садик (мир ему) советует своему сподвижнику принять богобоязненность (таква) в качестве основы духовной жизни. Имам указывает на опасное нравственное противоречие: тем, кто боится чужих грехов, но считает себя защищённым от последствий собственных. Рай Аллаха достигается лишь искренним повиновением.
summaries.tr: İmam Sadık (a.s.) bu mektupta bir sahabesine takvanın önemine dair nasihat etmektedir. İmam, başkalarının günahlarını kınayıp kendi günahlarının sonuçlarından emin olan kimselerin düştüğü tehlikeli ahlaki çelişkiye dikkat çekmektedir. Allah'ın cenneti, yalnızca gerçek itaatle kazanılabilir.
summaries.ur: امام جعفر صادق علیہ السلام نے اپنے ایک صحابی کو خط کے ذریعے تقویٰ اپنانے کی وصیت فرمائی۔ آپ نے اس روحانی تناقض کی نشاندہی فرمائی کہ ایک شخص دوسروں کے گناہوں کی تو فکر کرے مگر اپنے گناہوں سے بے پرواہ رہے — یہ اخلاقی منافقت ہے جو حقیقی ایمان سے مطابقت نہیں رکھتی۔ جنت صرف اطاعتِ الٰہی سے ہی ممکن ہے۔
summaries.zh: 在这封信中，伊玛目萨迪格（安主福之）建议一位弟子以塔格瓦（敬畏主）作为精神生活的基础。伊玛目揭示了一种危险的道德悖论：那些担心他人罪行的人，却对自己罪行的后果毫无顾虑。安拉的乐园只能通过真诚的服从来获得。

translation.en: I advise you / I enjoin upon you
and He will provide for him

translation.fa: اما خود در امان باشد از
translation.fr: mais se sent à l'abri de
translation.ru: но сам чувствует себя в безопасности от

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5 / 106)
text: قَرَأْتُ جَوَاباً مِنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (عليه السلام) إِلَى رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ

أَمَّا بَعْدُ فَإِنِّي أُوصِيكَ بِتَقْوَى اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ ضَمِنَ لِمَنِ اتَّقَاهُ أَنْ يُحَوِّلَهُ عَمَّا يَكْرَهُ إِلَى مَا يُحِبُّ وَ يَرْزُقَهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ فَإِيَّاكَ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِمَّنْ يَخَافُ عَلَى الْعِبَادِ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِمْ وَ يَأْمَنُ الْعُقُوبَةَ مِنْ ذَنْبِهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَا يُخْدَعُ عَنْ جَنَّتِهِ وَ لَا يُنَالُ مَا عِنْدَهُ إِلَّا بِطَاعَتِهِ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ .

en.hubeali: H 14457 – Ali Bin Muhammad, from someone he has mentioned, from Muhammad Bin Al-Husayn, and Humeyd Bin Zyad, from Al-Hassan Bin Muhammad Al-Kindy altogether, from Ahmad Bin Al-Hassan Al-Maysami, from a man from his companions who said:
‘I read the answer from Abu Abdullahasws to a man from hisasws companions.

After this (having Praised Allah<sup>azwj</sup>,), I<sup>asws</sup> hereby advise you all to fear Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, for He<sup>azwj</sup> has Taken the responsibility for the one who is pious to turn them around from what He<sup>azwj</sup> Abhors towards what He<sup>azwj</sup> Loves, and Sustain him from where he does not expect. So beware of becoming from those who are feared by the servants due to their sins whereas they themselves feel secure from the consequences of their own sins. Allah<sup>azwj</sup> cannot be deceived about His<sup>azwj</sup> Paradise, nor can that be achieved which is with Him<sup>azwj</sup> except by being obedient to Him<sup>azwj</sup>, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Willing’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali Bin Muhammad, from someone he has mentioned, from Muhammad Bin Al-Husayn, and Humeyd Bin Zyad, from Al-Hassan Bin Muhammad Al-Kindy altogether, from Ahmad Bin Al-Hassan Al-Maysami, from a man from his companions who said: ‘I read the answer from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) to a man from his (a.s.) companions. After this (having Praised Allah (azwj),), I (a.s.) hereby advise you all to fear Allah (azwj), for He (azwj) has Taken the responsibility for the one who is pious to turn them around from what He (azwj) Abhors towards what He (azwj) Loves, and Sustain him from where he does not expect. So beware of becoming from those who are feared by the servants due to their sins whereas they themselves feel secure from the consequences of their own sins. Allah (azwj) cannot be deceived about His (azwj) Paradise, nor can that be achieved which is with Him (azwj) except by being obedient to Him (azwj), Allah (azwj) Willing’.

translations.bn: আমাদের কিছু সংখ্যক সাথী থেকে, সাহল ইবনে যিয়াদ থেকে, মুহাম্মাদ ইবনে সুলাইমান থেকে, আইথাম ইবনে আশইয়াম থেকে, মুআবিয়া ইবনে আম্মার থেকে, আবু আবদিল্লাহ (আলাইহিস সালাম) থেকে বর্ণিত:
translations.de: Eine Anzahl unserer Gefährten, von Sahl ibn Ziyad, von Muhammad ibn Sulayman, von Aytham ibn Ashyam, von Mu'awiya ibn Ammar, von Abu Abdillah (Friede sei mit ihm), der sagte:
translations.en: A number of our companions, from Sahl ibn Ziyad, from Muhammad ibn Sulayman, from Aytham ibn Ashyam, from Mu'awiya ibn Ammar, from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said:
translations.es: Un grupo de nuestros compañeros, de Sahl ibn Ziyad, de Muhammad ibn Sulayman, de Aytham ibn Ashyam, de Mu'awiya ibn Ammar, de Abu Abdillah (la paz sea con él), quien dijo:
translations.fa: عده‌ای از اصحاب ما، از سهل بن زیاد، از محمد بن سلیمان، از عیثم بن اشیم، از معاویه بن عمار، از ابوعبدالله علیه السلام روایت کرده‌اند که:
translations.fr: Un groupe de nos compagnons, de Sahl ibn Ziyad, de Muhammad ibn Sulayman, de Aytham ibn Ashyam, de Mu'awiya ibn Ammar, d'Abu Abdillah (que la paix soit sur lui), qui a dit :
translations.id: Sejumlah sahabat kami, dari Sahl ibn Ziyad, dari Muhammad ibn Sulayman, dari Aytham ibn Ashyam, dari Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, dari Abu Abdillah (a.s.) yang meriwayatkan:
translations.ru: Группа наших сподвижников, от Сахла ибн Зийада, от Мухаммада ибн Сулеймана, от Айсама ибн Ашйама, от Муавийи ибн Аммара, от Абу Абдиллы (мир ему), который сказал:
translations.tr: Ashabımızdan bir grup, Sehl b. Ziyad'dan, Muhammed b. Süleyman'dan, Aytem b. Eşyem'den, Muaviye b. Ammar'dan, Ebu Abdillah (a.s.)'dan şunu nakletti:
translations.ur: ہمارے اصحاب کی ایک جماعت سے، سہل بن زیاد سے، محمد بن سلیمان سے، عیثم بن اشیم سے، معاویہ بن عمار سے، ابو عبداللہ علیہ السلام سے روایت ہے کہ:
translations.zh: 我们的一些同伴，从萨赫勒·伊本·齐亚德，从穆罕默德·伊本·苏莱曼，从艾萨姆·伊本·阿什彦，从穆阿维叶·伊本·阿玛尔，从阿布·阿卜迪拉（愿主赐他平安）传述：

translations.bn: একদিন নবী (সাল্লাল্লাহু আলাইহি ওয়া আলিহি) সুসংবাদ পেয়ে আনন্দিত ও হাস্যোজ্জ্বল হয়ে বের হলেন। মানুষেরা তাঁকে বলল: 'আল্লাহ আপনাকে সদা হাসিমুখ রাখুন, হে আল্লাহর রাসূল, এবং আপনার আনন্দ বৃদ্ধি করুন।'
translations.de: Eines Tages trat der Prophet (Allahs Segen und Friede seien auf ihm und seiner Familie) heraus und freute sich über eine gute Nachricht, lachend vor Freude. Die Menschen sagten zu ihm: 'Möge Gott dich stets lachen lassen, o Gesandter Gottes, und deine Freude mehren.'
translations.en: One day the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) came out having received good news, smiling with joy. So the people said to him: 'May God keep you smiling, O Messenger of God, and increase your joy.'
translations.es: Un día el Profeta (la paz sea con él y su familia) salió habiendo recibido buenas noticias, sonriendo con alegría. La gente le dijo: 'Que Dios te haga sonreír siempre, oh Mensajero de Dios, y que aumente tu alegría.'
translations.fa: روزی پیامبر صلی الله علیه و آله بیرون آمدند در حالی که مژده‌گیر و خندان از روی شادی بودند. مردم به ایشان گفتند: خداوند دندانت را بخنداند ای رسول خدا و شادیت را افزون کند.
translations.fr: Un jour le Prophète (que la paix et les bénédictions d'Allah soient sur lui et sa famille) sortit ayant reçu une bonne nouvelle, souriant de joie. Les gens lui dirent : 'Qu'Allah te fasse toujours sourire, ô Messager d'Allah, et qu'Il augmente ta joie.'
translations.id: Suatu hari Nabi (s.a.w.w.) keluar dalam keadaan bersuka cita menerima kabar gembira, tersenyum penuh kebahagiaan. Maka orang-orang berkata kepadanya: 'Semoga Allah senantiasa membuatmu tersenyum wahai Rasulullah, dan menambahkan kegembiraanmu.'
translations.ru: Однажды Пророк (да благословит его Аллах и его семья) вышел, исполненный радости от благой вести, улыбаясь от счастья. Люди сказали ему: 'Да сделает тебя Аллах всегда улыбающимся, о Посланник Аллаха, и да умножит Он твою радость.'
translations.tr: Bir gün Peygamber (s.a.v.s.) müjdeli bir haberle sevinçli ve gülerek dışarı çıktı. İnsanlar ona dediler: 'Allah seni güldürsün ey Allah'ın Resulü, sevincinizi artırsın.'
translations.ur: ایک دن نبی صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ باہر تشریف لائے اور وہ خوشخبری سے خوش و خرم ہنستے ہوئے تھے۔ لوگوں نے کہا: اللہ آپ کو ہنساتا رہے اے اللہ کے رسول، اور آپ کی خوشی بڑھاتا رہے۔
translations.zh: 有一天，先知（愿主赐福于他及其家族）带着喜讯出来，因喜悦而面带笑容。众人对他说：「愿安拉使你永远欢笑，哦安拉的使者，并增加你的喜悦。」

translations.bn: আল্লাহর রাসূল (সাল্লাল্লাহু আলাইহি ওয়া আলিহি) বললেন: 'এমন কোনো দিন বা রাত নেই যেখানে আমি আল্লাহর পক্ষ থেকে একটি উপহার পাই না। সাবধান! আমার পালনকর্তা আজকের এই দিনে আমাকে এমন একটি উপহার দিয়েছেন যা আগে কখনও দেননি। জিবরাঈল আমার কাছে এলেন এবং আমার পালনকর্তার পক্ষ থেকে সালাম পৌঁছালেন এবং বললেন: হে মুহাম্মাদ!
translations.de: Der Gesandte Gottes (Allahs Segen und Friede seien auf ihm und seiner Familie) sagte: 'Es gibt keinen Tag und keine Nacht, ohne dass ich von Gott ein Geschenk in beiden empfange. Wisset! Mein Herr hat mir an diesem meinem Tag ein Geschenk gegeben, dessen Gleichen Er mir nie zuvor gegeben hat. Gabriel kam zu mir und übermittelte mir die Grüße meines Herrn und sagte: O Muhammad!
translations.en: The Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) said: 'There is no day or night except that I receive a gift from God in both. Indeed! My Lord has gifted me on this day of mine with a gift the like of which He has never gifted me before. Gabriel came to me and conveyed to me greetings from my Lord, and said: O Muhammad!
translations.es: El Mensajero de Dios (la paz sea con él y su familia) dijo: 'No hay un día ni una noche excepto que yo recibo un regalo de Dios en ambos. ¡Sabed! Mi Señor me ha regalado en este mi día un regalo del cual nunca me ha dado nada semejante antes. Gabriel vino a mí y me transmitió los saludos de mi Señor, y dijo: ¡Oh Muhammad!
translations.fa: رسول خدا صلی الله علیه و آله فرمودند: هیچ روز و شبی نیست مگر اینکه در آن دو، هدیه‌ای از خداوند برای من است. آگاه باشید! پروردگارم در این روزم هدیه‌ای به من داد که مانند آن را هرگز به من نداده بود. جبرئیل نزدم آمد و از جانب پروردگارم سلام رساند و گفت: ای محمد!
translations.fr: Le Messager d'Allah (que la paix et les bénédictions d'Allah soient sur lui et sa famille) dit : 'Il n'y a pas de jour ni de nuit sans que je reçoive un cadeau d'Allah en eux deux. Sachez ! Mon Seigneur m'a offert en ce jour un cadeau dont Il ne m'avait jamais rien offert de semblable auparavant. Jibrîl me vint et me transmit les salutations de mon Seigneur, et dit : Ô Muhammad !
translations.id: Rasulullah (s.a.w.w.) bersabda: 'Tiada satu hari pun dan satu malam pun kecuali aku menerima hadiah dari Allah di dalamnya. Ketahuilah! Tuhanku telah memberiku hadiah pada hariku ini sebuah hadiah yang belum pernah diberikan-Nya yang serupa sebelumnya. Jibril datang kepadaku dan menyampaikan salam dari Tuhanku, lalu berkata: Wahai Muhammad!
translations.ru: Посланник Аллаха (да благословит его Аллах и его семья) сказал: 'Нет такого дня и ночи, чтобы я не получил от Аллаха подарка в оба. Воистину! Мой Господь одарил меня в этот мой день даром, подобного которому Он никогда прежде мне не давал. Джибриль пришёл ко мне и передал мне приветствие от моего Господа, и сказал: О Мухаммад!
translations.tr: Allah'ın Resulü (s.a.v.s.) buyurdu: 'Geçen her gün ve gecede Allah'tan bir hediye almaktayım. Dikkat edin! Rabbim bugün bana daha önce hiç vermediği bir hediye verdi. Cebrail bana geldi ve Rabbimden selam iletti ve dedi: Ey Muhammed!
translations.ur: اللہ کے رسول صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ نے فرمایا: کوئی دن اور رات ایسا نہیں گزرتا جس میں میرے لیے اللہ کی طرف سے کوئی تحفہ نہ ہو۔ آگاہ رہو، میرے رب نے مجھے آج کے دن ایک ایسا تحفہ دیا ہے جو مجھے اس سے پہلے کبھی نہیں دیا گیا تھا۔ جبرئیل میرے پاس آئے اور میرے رب کی طرف سے سلام پہنچایا اور کہا: اے محمد!
translations.zh: 安拉的使者（愿主赐福于他及其家族）说：「没有一天或一夜，我没有从安拉那里收到礼物。注意！我的主在我今天赐予了我一份前所未有的礼物。吉卜里勒来到我这里，从我的主那里带来了问候，并说：哦穆罕默德！

translations.bn: আল্লাহ আজ্জা ওয়া জাল্লা বনু হাশিম থেকে সাতজনকে মনোনীত করেছেন, অতীতে যাদের মতো কাউকে সৃষ্টি করা হয়নি এবং ভবিষ্যতেও করা হবে না। তুমি হে আল্লাহর রাসূল, নবীদের নেতা; আলী ইবনে আবি তালিব তোমার উত্তরসূরি এবং উত্তরসূরিদের নেতা; হাসান ও হুসাইন তোমার দুই নাতি এবং নাতিদের দুই নেতা; হামযা তোমার চাচা এবং শহীদদের নেতা; জাফর তোমার চাচার পুত্র এবং জান্নাতের উড়নকারী যিনি ফেরেশতাদের সাথে যেখানে ইচ্ছা উড়ে বেড়ান; এবং তোমাদের মধ্যে থেকে আল-কায়িম আসবেন যাঁর পিছনে ঈসা ইবনে মারিয়াম নামায পড়বেন যখন আল্লাহ তাঁকে পৃথিবীতে নামিয়ে দেবেন — তিনি আলী ও ফাতিমার বংশ থেকে, হুসাইনের (আলাইহিস সালাম) সন্তানদের থেকে।'
translations.de: Gott, der Mächtige und Majestätische, hat sieben aus dem Clan der Hashim auserwählt, deren Gleichen Er weder unter denen geschaffen hat, die vergangen sind, noch schaffen wird unter denen, die noch kommen. Du, o Gesandter Gottes, bist der Anführer der Propheten; Ali ibn Abi Talib, dein Nachfolger, ist der Anführer der Nachfolger; al-Hasan und al-Husayn, deine zwei Enkel, sind die zwei Anführer der Enkel; Hamza, dein Onkel, ist der Anführer der Märtyrer; Ja'far, der Sohn deines Onkels, ist der Flieger im Paradies, der mit den Engeln fliegt wohin er will; und aus eurer Mitte ist der Qa'im, hinter dem Isa ibn Maryam beten wird, wenn Gott ihn auf die Erde herabsendet — er wird aus der Nachkommenschaft von Ali und Fatima sein, aus den Kindern von al-Husayn (Friede sei mit ihm).'
translations.en: God, Mighty and Majestic, has chosen seven from the Clan of Hashim the like of whom He has never created in those who have passed, nor will He create their like among those who remain. You, O Messenger of God, are the Chief of the Prophets; and Ali ibn Abi Talib, your successor, is the Chief of the successors; and al-Hasan and al-Husayn, your two grandsons, are the two Chiefs of the grandsons; and Hamza, your uncle, is the Chief of the martyrs; and Ja'far, the son of your uncle, is the Flyer in Paradise, flying with the angels wherever he pleases; and from among you is the Qa'im behind whom Isa ibn Maryam will pray when God sends him down to the earth — he will be from the descendants of Ali and Fatima, from the children of al-Husayn (peace be upon him).'
translations.es: Dios, Poderoso y Majestuoso, ha elegido a siete del Clan de Hashim de los cuales nunca ha creado a sus semejantes entre quienes han pasado, ni creará sus semejantes entre quienes quedan. Tú, oh Mensajero de Dios, eres el Jefe de los Profetas; Ali ibn Abi Talib, tu sucesor, es el Jefe de los sucesores; al-Hasan y al-Husayn, tus dos nietos, son los dos Jefes de los nietos; Hamza, tu tío, es el Jefe de los mártires; Ja'far, el hijo de tu tío, es el Volador en el Paraíso, volando con los ángeles adonde desea; y de entre vosotros es el Qa'im detrás del cual Isa ibn Maryam orará cuando Dios lo envíe a la tierra — él será de la descendencia de Ali y Fatima, de los hijos de al-Husayn (la paz sea con él).'
translations.fa: خداوند عزوجل از بنی هاشم هفت تن را برگزیده که نه در گذشتگان مانندشان آفریده و نه در آیندگان مانندشان خواهد آفرید. تو ای رسول خدا سرور پیامبرانی؛ علی بن ابی طالب وصی تو و سرور اوصیاست؛ حسن و حسین دو نوه‌ات و دو سرور اسباطند؛ حمزه عموی تو سرور شهیدان است؛ جعفر پسر عموی تو طیار در بهشت است که با فرشتگان هر کجا بخواهد پرواز می‌کند؛ و از شما قائم است که هنگامی که خداوند عیسی بن مریم را به زمین فرو فرستد عیسی پشت سر او نماز می‌گزارد — از ذریه علی و فاطمه، از فرزندان حسین علیه السلام.
translations.fr: Allah, Puissant et Majestueux, a choisi sept parmi le Clan de Hachim dont Il n'a jamais créé de semblables parmi ceux qui ont passé, et ne créera pas leurs semblables parmi ceux qui restent. Toi, ô Messager d'Allah, tu es le Chef des prophètes ; Ali ibn Abi Talib ton successeur est le Chef des successeurs ; al-Hasan et al-Husayn tes deux petits-fils sont les deux Chefs des petits-fils ; Hamza ton oncle est le Chef des martyrs ; Ja'far le fils de ton oncle est le Volant dans le Paradis, volant avec les anges où il le désire ; et parmi vous est le Qa'im derrière lequel Îsâ ibn Maryam priera quand Allah l'enverra sur terre — il sera de la descendance d'Ali et Fatima, parmi les enfants d'al-Husayn (que la paix soit sur lui).'
translations.id: Allah Azza wa Jalla telah memilih tujuh orang dari Bani Hasyim yang belum pernah Dia ciptakan yang serupa di antara orang-orang yang telah berlalu, dan tidak akan Dia ciptakan yang serupa di antara orang-orang yang akan datang. Engkau wahai Rasulullah adalah pemimpin para nabi; Ali ibn Abi Thalib adalah penerusmu dan pemimpin para penerus; al-Hasan dan al-Husayn dua cucumu adalah dua pemimpin cucu-cucu; Hamzah pamanmu adalah pemimpin para syahid; Ja'far anak pamanmu adalah penerbang di surga yang terbang bersama malaikat ke mana pun ia kehendaki; dan dari kalian adalah al-Qa'im yang di belakangnya Isa ibn Maryam akan shalat ketika Allah menurunkannya ke bumi — ia berasal dari keturunan Ali dan Fatimah, dari anak-anak al-Husayn (a.s.).'
translations.ru: Аллах, Могущественный и Величественный, избрал семь из рода Хашим, подобных которым Он не создавал среди тех, кто ушёл, и не создаст подобных им среди тех, кто останется. Ты, о Посланник Аллаха, — Господин пророков; Али ибн Аби Талиб, твой преемник, — Господин преемников; аль-Хасан и аль-Хусайн, твои два внука, — два Господина внуков; Хамза, твой дядя, — Господин мучеников; Джа'фар, сын твоего дяди, — Летящий в Раю, летящий с ангелами куда пожелает; и из вас аль-Каим, позади которого будет молиться Иса ибн Марьям, когда Аллах ниспошлёт его на землю — он будет из потомков Али и Фатимы, из детей аль-Хусайна (мир ему).'
translations.tr: Allah Azze ve Celle, Haşimoğulları'ndan geçmişte eşi yaratılmamış ve gelecekte de eşi yaratılmayacak yedi kişiyi seçti. Sen ey Allah'ın Resulü peygamberlerin efendisisin; Ali b. Ebi Talib vasın ve vasıların efendisidir; Hasan ve Hüseyin iki torunun ve esbaların iki efendisidir; amcan Hamza şehitlerin efendisidir; amcanın oğlu Cafer cennette uçan, meleklerle dilediği yere uçandır; ve sizden el-Kaim gelecektir ki Allah onu yere indirdiğinde İsa b. Meryem onun arkasında namaz kılacaktır — o Ali ve Fatıma'nın soyundan, Hüseyin'in (a.s.) çocuklarından olacaktır.'
translations.ur: اللہ عزوجل نے بنی ہاشم میں سے سات افراد کو منتخب کیا ہے جن جیسا نہ پہلے کسی کو پیدا کیا اور نہ آئندہ کریں گے۔ آپ اے اللہ کے رسول تمام نبیوں کے سردار ہیں، علی بن ابی طالب آپ کے وصی اور تمام اوصیاء کے سردار ہیں، حسن اور حسین آپ کے دونوں نواسے اور تمام اسباط کے سردار ہیں، حمزہ آپ کے چچا اور تمام شہداء کے سردار ہیں، جعفر آپ کے چچا کے بیٹے جنت میں اڑنے والے ہیں فرشتوں کے ساتھ جہاں چاہیں اڑتے ہیں۔ اور تم میں سے القائم ہوں گے جن کے پیچھے عیسیٰ بن مریم نماز پڑھیں گے جب اللہ انہیں زمین پر اتارے گا، علی اور فاطمہ کی ذریت سے، حسین علیہ السلام کی اولاد سے۔
translations.zh: 安拉——威严而崇高——从哈希姆族中选择了七人，过去从未创造过他们的同类，将来也不会创造。你，哦安拉的使者，是众先知的领袖；阿里·伊本·艾布·塔利布是你的继承人，是众继承人的领袖；哈桑和侯赛因是你的两个孙儿，是众孙儿的两位领袖；哈木扎，你的叔父，是众殉道者的领袖；贾法尔，你叔父之子，是天堂中的飞翔者，与天使们飞往他所愿的任何地方；而在你们之中有卡伊姆，当安拉将他降至大地时，尔萨·伊本·麦尔彦将在他身后祈祷——他将来自阿里和法蒂玛的后裔，来自侯赛因（愿主赐他平安）的子嗣。」

diacritics_changes: Added full tashkeel to narrator chain
Added full tashkeel to main narrative body
Added diacritics to عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ and صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ عَيْثَمِ بْنِ أَشْيَمَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: خَرَجَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ وَهُوَ مُسْتَبْشِرٌ يَضْحَكُ سُرُوراً فَقَالَ لَهُ النَّاسُ أَضْحَكَ اللَّهُ سِنَّكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَزَادَكَ سُرُوراً فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنْ يَوْمٍ وَلَا لَيْلَةٍ إِلَّا وَلِيَ فِيهِمَا تُحْفَةٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ أَلَا وَإِنَّ رَبِّي أَتْحَفَنِي فِي يَوْمِي هَذَا بِتُحْفَةٍ لَمْ يُتْحِفْنِي بِمِثْلِهَا فِيمَا مَضَى إِنَّ جَبْرَئِيلَ أَتَانِي فَأَقْرَأَنِي مِنْ رَبِّيَ السَّلَامَ وَقَالَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ اخْتَارَ مِنْ بَنِي هَاشِمٍ سَبْعَةً لَمْ يَخْلُقْ مِثْلَهُمْ فِيمَنْ مَضَى وَلَا يَخْلُقُ مِثْلَهُمْ فِيمَنْ بَقِيَ أَنْتَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ سَيِّدُ النَّبِيِّينَ وَعَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ وَصِيُّكَ سَيِّدُ الْوَصِيِّينَ وَالْحَسَنُ وَالْحُسَيْنُ سِبْطَاكَ سَيِّدَا الْأَسْبَاطِ وَحَمْزَةُ عَمُّكَ سَيِّدُ الشُّهَدَاءِ وَجَعْفَرٌ ابْنُ عَمِّكَ الطَّيَّارُ فِي الْجَنَّةِ يَطِيرُ مَعَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ حَيْثُ يَشَاءُ وَمِنْكُمُ الْقَائِمُ يُصَلِّي عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ خَلْفَهُ إِذَا أَهْبَطَهُ اللَّهُ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ عَلِيٍّ وَفَاطِمَةَ مِنْ وُلْدِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ

narrators.ambiguity_note: Standard Kulayni opening formula referring to multiple unnamed transmitters

narrators.known_identity: Sahl ibn Ziyad al-Adami al-Razi, a Shia hadith transmitter in al-Kafi

narrators.ambiguity_note: Possibly Muhammad ibn Sulayman al-Daylami, a transmitter in several hadith chains
Relatively unknown narrator; may be a variant spelling

narrators.known_identity: Mu'awiya ibn Ammar al-Duhni, a prominent Shia narrator and companion of Imam al-Sadiq
Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam

key_phrases.phrase_en: The Rising One from the family of Muhammad (the Mahdi)

bn.الطَّيَّارُ: আত-তাইয়ার (উড়নকারী) — জাফর ইবনে আবি তালিবের উপাধি যিনি মুতার যুদ্ধে শহীদ হয়েছিলেন এবং জান্নাতে পাখার পরিবর্তে হাত পেয়েছিলেন
bn.سِبْطَاكَ: আপনার দুই নাতি — হাসান ও হুসাইনকে নির্দেশ করে

de.الطَّيَّارُ: Der Flieger — Beiname von Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, der in der Schlacht von Mu'ta beide Arme verlor und dem im Paradies Flügel verliehen wurden
de.الْقَائِمُ: Der Qa'im — Der Aufstehende, Titel des Imam Mahdi in der schiitischen Tradition
de.وَصِيُّكَ: Dein Nachfolger — bestimmt Ali als designierten Nachfolger des Propheten

en.الطَّيَّارُ: The Flyer — the epithet of Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, who lost both arms at the Battle of Mu'ta and was said to be granted wings in Paradise
en.الْقَائِمُ: The Qa'im — the Rising One, a title for the awaited Mahdi in Shia tradition
en.تُحْفَةٌ: Gift — used three times to emphasize the extraordinary nature of the divine bestowal
en.سِبْطَاكَ: Your two grandsons (sibtayk) — specifically referring to al-Hasan and al-Husayn as sons of the Prophet's daughter Fatima
en.وَصِيُّكَ: Your successor/legatee — establishing Ali as the designated successor of the Prophet

es.الطَّيَّارُ: El Volador — epíteto de Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, quien perdió ambos brazos en la Batalla de Mu'ta y se dice que fue recompensado con alas en el Paraíso
es.الْقَائِمُ: El Qa'im — el que se levanta, título del Imam Mahdi en la tradición chií
es.وَصِيُّكَ: Tu sucesor/legatario — estableciendo a Alí como el sucesor designado del Profeta

fa.الطَّيَّارُ: طیار — لقب جعفر بن ابی طالب که در نبرد موته شهید شد و در بهشت دو بال عوض دو دستش به او داده شد
fa.الْقَائِمُ: قائم — لقب امام مهدی علیه السلام
fa.سِبْطَاكَ: دو نوه تو — اشاره به حسن و حسین علیهما السلام که از نسل دختر پیامبر حضرت فاطمه علیها السلام هستند

fr.الطَّيَّارُ: Le Volant — épithète de Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, qui perdit ses deux bras à la bataille de Mu'ta et reçut des ailes au Paradis
fr.الْقَائِمُ: Le Qa'im — Celui qui se lève, titre de l'Imam Mahdi dans la tradition chiite
fr.سِبْطَاكَ: Tes deux petits-fils — désignant al-Hasan et al-Husayn comme fils de la fille du Prophète, Fatima

id.الطَّيَّارُ: Al-Thayyar (Penerbang) — gelar Ja'far ibn Abi Thalib yang gugur di Pertempuran Mu'tah dan dikisahkan mendapat sayap di surga
id.الْقَائِمُ: Al-Qa'im — Yang Bangkit, gelar Imam Mahdi
id.وَصِيُّكَ: Penerusmu — menetapkan Ali sebagai suksesor yang ditunjuk Nabi

ru.الطَّيَّارُ: Летящий (ат-Тайяр) — эпитет Джа'фара ибн Аби Талиба, потерявшего обе руки в битве при Му'те, которому в Раю были дарованы крылья
ru.الْقَائِمُ: аль-Каим — Восстающий, титул имама Махди в шиитской традиции
ru.سِبْطَاكَ: Твои два внука — аль-Хасан и аль-Хусайн как сыновья дочери Пророка Фатимы

tr.الطَّيَّارُ: Et-Tayyar — uçan anlamında, Cafer b. Ebi Talib'in lakabı; Mute Savaşı'nda şehit olan ve cennette kanatlar verilen sahabi
tr.الْقَائِمُ: El-Kaim — ayağa kalkan, Mehdi için kullanılan bir lakat
tr.وَصِيُّكَ: Vasin — vasiyetini üstlenen, burada Hz. Ali'nin Peygamber'in halefi olduğunu belirtir

ur.الطَّيَّارُ: طیار — جعفر بن ابی طالب کا لقب جنہیں جنگ موتہ میں دونوں ہاتھ کٹ جانے کے بعد جنت میں پر عطا کیے گئے
ur.تُحْفَةٌ: تحفہ — تین بار استعمال سے اس الٰہی عطا کی اہمیت واضح ہوتی ہے

zh.الطَّيَّارُ: 飞翔者（泰亚尔）——贾法尔·伊本·艾布·塔利布的称号，他在穆塔战役中失去双臂，据传在天堂中获赐双翼
zh.الْقَائِمُ: 卡伊姆——起义者，什叶派传统中伊玛目马赫迪的称号
zh.سِبْطَاكَ: 你的两个孙儿——哈桑和侯赛因，作为先知之女法蒂玛之子

seo_questions.bn: আল-কাফিতে উল্লিখিত বনু হাশিমের সাতজন মনোনীত ব্যক্তি কারা?
seo_questions.de: Wer sind die sieben Auserwählten aus Banu Hashim, die in al-Kafi erwähnt werden?
seo_questions.en: What are the seven chosen ones from Banu Hashim mentioned in al-Kafi?
seo_questions.es: ¿Quiénes son los siete elegidos de Banu Hashim mencionados en al-Kafi?
seo_questions.fa: هفت تن برگزیده از بنی هاشم در الکافی چه کسانی هستند؟
seo_questions.fr: Qui sont les sept élus de Banu Hâchim mentionnés dans al-Kafi ?
seo_questions.id: Siapa tujuh orang pilihan dari Bani Hasyim yang disebutkan dalam al-Kafi?
seo_questions.ru: Кто такие семь избранных из рода Хашим, упомянутых в аль-Кафи?
seo_questions.tr: El-Kafi'deki Haşimoğulları'ndan seçilen yedi kişi kimlerdir?
seo_questions.ur: الکافی میں بنی ہاشم کے سات منتخب افراد کون ہیں؟
seo_questions.zh: 《卡非》中提到的哈希姆家族七位被选中者是谁？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths about the virtues and unique station of the Ahlulbayt as selected by God, often listing their specific titles and ranks among all creation.
Traditions about Ja'far ibn Abi Talib al-Tayyar and his special status in Paradise of flying with the angels, often in the context of hadiths about martyrs and their rewards.
Eschatological hadiths about the Qa'im/Mahdi and the descent of Jesus son of Mary, who will pray behind the Mahdi when he emerges at the end of times.

summaries.bn: এই হাদীসটি ইমাম জাফর আস-সাদিক (আ.) থেকে বর্ণিত, যেখানে নবী মুহাম্মাদ (সাঃ) একদিন আনন্দিত হয়ে বের হলেন এবং কারণ জিজ্ঞেস করলে জানালেন যে জিবরাঈল সর্বোচ্চ উপহার নিয়ে এসেছেন: বনু হাশিম থেকে সাত অনন্য ব্যক্তিত্বের ঐশী মনোনয়নের ঘোষণা। শিয়া ঐতিহ্যে এই হাদীস আহলুল বাইতের অতুলনীয় মর্যাদা প্রমাণ করে এবং ইমাম মাহদী ও ঈসা (আ.)-এর আগমনের ভবিষ্যদ্বাণী বহন করে।
summaries.de: Dieser Hadith berichtet über Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (F), dass der Prophet Muhammad (F) eines Tages freudestrahlend heraustrat und auf Nachfrage offenbarte, dass Gabriel die größte Gabe überbracht hatte: die göttliche Bekanntmachung sieben einzigartiger Auserwählter aus Banu Hashim. In der schiitischen Tradition ist dieser Hadith bedeutsam, da er den unvergleichlichen kosmischen Rang der Ahl al-Bayt belegt und eine eschatologische Referenz auf den Mahdi und die Wiederkehr Jesu enthält.
summaries.en: This hadith narrates that Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS) reported that the Prophet Muhammad (SAWW) once emerged joyful, and when asked why, he revealed that Gabriel had brought him the greatest gift he had ever received: a divine proclamation naming seven uniquely chosen figures from Banu Hashim. These seven are: the Prophet as Chief of Prophets, Ali ibn Abi Talib as Chief of Successors, al-Hasan and al-Husayn as chiefs of the grandsons, Hamza as Chief of Martyrs, Ja'far ibn Abi Talib as the Flyer in Paradise, and a future Qa'im from the lineage of Ali and Fatima from the children of al-Husayn, behind whom Jesus will pray upon his return. This hadith is significant in the Shia tradition as it establishes the unique cosmic rank of the Ahlulbayt and includes an eschatological reference to the Mahdi.
summaries.es: Este hadith narra a través de Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (P) que el Profeta Muhammad (BPD) salió un día jubiloso y, cuando se le preguntó, reveló que Gabriel le había traído el más grande regalo: el anuncio divino de siete figuras únicas elegidas de Banu Hashim. En la tradición chií, este hadith es fundamental pues establece el rango cósmico sin igual de los Ahlulbayt e incluye una referencia escatológica al Mahdi y al regreso de Jesús.
summaries.fa: این حدیث از امام جعفر صادق علیه السلام روایت شده است که پیامبر اکرم صلی الله علیه و آله روزی شادمان بیرون آمدند و هنگامی که دلیل پرسیده شد، فرمودند جبرئیل بزرگ‌ترین هدیه‌ای را که تاکنون دریافت کرده بودند آورده: اعلام انتخاب هفت تن بی‌همتا از بنی هاشم. این حدیث از نظر شیعه اهمیت بسیار دارد چرا که مقام منحصربه‌فرد اهل‌بیت را بیان کرده و آمدن قائم علیه السلام و نماز خواندن عیسی علیه السلام پشت سر ایشان را نوید می‌دهد.
summaries.fr: Ce hadith rapporté par l'Imam Ja'far al-Sâdiq (P) relate que le Prophète Muhammad (P) sortit un jour rayonnant et, lorsqu'on l'interrogea, révéla que Jibrîl lui avait apporté le plus grand cadeau jamais reçu : l'annonce divine de sept personnages uniques élus de Banu Hâchim. Dans la tradition chiite, ce hadith est fondamental car il établit le rang cosmique inégalé des Ahl al-Bayt et inclut une référence eschatologique au Mahdi et au retour d'Isa.
summaries.id: Hadis ini diriwayatkan dari Imam Ja'far al-Shadiq (a.s.) bahwa Nabi Muhammad (s.a.w.w.) pernah keluar dengan gembira dan ketika ditanya, beliau mengungkapkan bahwa Jibril telah membawa hadiah terbesar: pengumuman pemilihan tujuh orang istimewa dari Bani Hasyim. Ketujuh orang ini memiliki kedudukan kosmis yang unik dalam tradisi Syiah dan hadis ini mengandung referensi eskatologis tentang al-Mahdi dan kedatangan kembali Isa (a.s.).
summaries.ru: Этот хадис передаётся через имама Джа'фара ас-Садика (мир ему) о том, что Пророк Мухаммад (мир ему) однажды вышел радостным и, когда его спросили о причине, открыл, что Джибриль принёс ему наивеличайший дар: Божественное провозглашение об избрании семи уникальных фигур из рода Хашим. В шиитской традиции этот хадис имеет первостепенное значение, так как утверждает несравненное космическое положение Ахль аль-Байт и содержит эсхатологическое указание на Махди и возвращение Исы (мир ему).
summaries.tr: Bu hadis, İmam Cafer es-Sadık'ın (a.s.) rivayetiyle Hz. Peygamber'in (s.a.v.s.) sevinçle geldiği ve Cebrail'in Allah'tan gelen en büyük hediyeyi — Haşimoğulları'ndan eşsiz yedi seçilmiş ismin ilanını — haber verdiği anlatılmaktadır.
